title: Cyclosorus albidipilosus
taxonomy: Cyclosorus albidipilosus is classified within the family Thelypteridaceae, which is a family of ferns commonly known as the marsh ferns or thelypterid ferns. This fern is further categorized as a species under the genus Cyclosorus. The classification also includes its rank as a species, Cyclosorus albidipilosus. Within the kingdom Plantae, Cyclosorus albidipilosus is classified as a vascular plant and a member of the division Pteridophyta. The species is also recognized as a terrestrial plant, growing in various environments such as forests and grasslands.
uses: Cyclosorus albidipilosus has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. Its leaves are used to treat fever, cough, and other respiratory issues. In some parts of the world, it is used to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. The plant is also believed to have antimicrobial properties, helping to combat infections. However, more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal uses and potential benefits.

intro: Cyclosorus albidipilosus, also known as the White-velled Lady fern, is a type of fern plant that belongs to the family Blechnaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in reducing fever, treating wounds, and preventing infections. The bioactive constituents of Cyclosorus albidipilosus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
